items.title: Treasury yields surge as markets brace for payrolls data.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino of Path Trading Partners analyzes the morning's market movements as U.S. Treasury yields and the dollar move higher while equity futures show early weakness. He discusses recent commentary on inflation, expectations surrounding the upcoming U.S. nonfarm payrolls report, and corporate earnings previews.

items.title: Tour de France: the 7 kg commodity basket behind the world's fastest bikes
items.excerpt: Every July, the Tour de France delivers one of sport's greatest spectacles. Over three gruelling weeks, the world's strongest cyclists race more than 3,300 kilometres across mountains, valleys and cobbled roads, at speeds once unimaginable. Fans marvel at the athletes, tactics and scenery. Equipment enthusiasts admire the increasingly futuristic machines beneath them. Commodity investors, however, might notice something else. A modern Tour de France bicycle is essentially a minimum 6.8 kilogram rolling commodity basket, dressed up as aerodynamics, marginal gains and sponsor logos. It may look deceptively simple - just two wheels connected by a carbon frame - but almost every visible component traces its origins back to a mine, an oil field, a natural gas processing plant or a chemical…

items.title: Q3 Outlook for Traders: AI’s challenging growth math and a new era at the Fed
items.excerpt: The AI growth trajectory, the Kevin Warsh Fed and Geopolitics Late Q2 saw a strong comeback in AI stocks, chiefly in the semiconductor and other hardware names that are most clearly enjoying or absorbing the spending from the staggering pace of capital expenditures to build out data center capacity. The coming quarter or two could see a slowing of these AI-linked hardware stocks as the market may question how far into the future projected growth rates can continue. Overall, this could mean a choppy equity market. Elsewhere, our focus in this quarterly outlook is on the new era set to begin at the Fed as Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has now taken charge and whether critical minerals and other commodities continue to represent an area of opportunity for traders. The challenging AI growth math:…
items.author: John J. Hardy

items.title: Euro futures held near 1.1420 as markets await Sintra forum.
items.excerpt: Todd Colvin from Mark IV Brokerage examines the recent price action in Euro futures, which closed near 1.1420 on the session. After hitting lows not seen in over a year at 1.1325 during the previous week, the currency has been steadily creeping higher to recover lost ground. Meanwhile, volatility remains relatively flat and unchanged, halting a downward trend observed over the past month and a half. Looking ahead, market participants are heavily focused on upcoming macroeconomic data, including the ADP employment change and ISM reports. More importantly, all eyes are turning to the European Central Bank forum in Sintra, Portugal, as traders await key testimony and insights from global central bankers including the ECB, the Bank of England, and the new Fed Chair, Kevin Warsh. Learn more…
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Q3 Outlook for Investors: Keep the future, hedge the crowd
items.excerpt: Q3 looks at balancing AI exposure in a top-heavy market. Key points: AI remains structural, but it is no longer one simple trade : Investors should stay exposed, but separate AI builders, users and efficiency winners because each faces different risks from capex, pricing, funding costs and proof of monetisation. Lower oil prices may bring relief, but the Fed is still constrained: A US Iran peace framework could ease inflation pressure, but sticky services, wages, tariffs and fiscal spending mean investors should still prioritise pricing power, cash flow and balance-sheet strength. Concentration is becoming the hidden portfolio risk: Market-cap indices have worked well, but they increasingly embed exposure to AI, mega-cap growth and momentum. Equal weight, quality defensives, income and…
items.author: Charu Chanana

items.title: Nasdaq leads stock futures rebound ahead of Fed Chair Warsh speech.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino breaks down the latest market shifts as Nasdaq futures lead an early morning turnaround. This commentary covers critical global economic data updates, including stronger U.S. labor numbers via JOLTS and Chinese manufacturing growth, alongside key central bank signals. Ahead of new U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's upcoming policy panel appearance at the ECB Forum, find out how the U.S. dollar, Treasury yields, and corporate earnings from major companies like Nike could impact your trading strategies.

items.title: Grains await USDA reality check after fund-driven round trip
items.excerpt: Key points: USDA acreage and quarterly grain stocks reports will determine whether June's sharp selloff has gone far enough or whether markets need to price even more supply comfort. Corn remains the main focus, with June 1 inventories expected to reach their highest level since 1988, helping offset concerns about Midwest heat and a potentially strong El Ni o later this year. Hedge funds have completed a remarkable positioning reversal, flipping from the largest combined long in corn, soybeans and wheat since 2022 to an outright net short in little more than a month The collapse in crude oil prices following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz has reduced an important source of support for biofuel-linked demand, particularly soybean oil. After surging nearly 17% up until mid-May on…

items.title: Euro futures rise to trim losses after testing one-year lows.
items.excerpt: Euro futures rebounded for a third consecutive session, rising approximately a third of a percent to trade at 1.1465. The recovery comes after a five-day losing streak pushed the currency to one-year lows. Market sentiment was supported by softening geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, with prospects for ceasefire extensions and further negotiations. Additionally, an uptick in Eurozone lending and banking activity provided a positive economic signal. Across FX markets, the Euro and the British pound exhibited the strongest relative performance, while the Japanese yen, Australian dollar, and Canadian dollar faced downward pressure.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Markets react to weekend strikes and pending rulings.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino of Path Trading Partners breaks down the morning's market activity as a strong Nasdaq leads equities, though small caps back off their highs. He analyzes the commodity space, highlighting WTI crude oil's jump past $70 following U.S. and Iranian military strikes over the weekend. Iaccino also previews potential Supreme Court rulings that could impact the Federal Reserve's independence and details the upcoming slate of global economic data, including U.S. home prices, consumer confidence, and JOLTS job openings.

items.title: Japanese Yen futures fell to log a 7th straight weekly loss.
items.excerpt: Japanese Yen futures experienced a minor daily bounce during a quiet trading session, yet the currency remains on track to register a seventh consecutive weekly decline. The primary structural headwind pressuring the yen continues to be the substantial interest rate differential between the U.S. and Japan. Even with the Bank of Japan raising its policy rate to 1% in mid-June, the wide gap relative to Federal Reserve interest rates keeps yen carry trades highly attractive to market participants. While rising wholesale prices related to Middle East tensions add some domestic tightening pressure, the overall interest rate gap remains a dominant factor. Furthermore, heavy government intervention spending totaling approximately 73 billion dollars between April and May has shown limited…
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Double-digit yield drops and quarter-end flows collide.
items.excerpt: The final week of June brings a rare convergence of month-end and quarter-end rebalancing, historically a catalyst for heightened equity market volatility. Traders are closely watching the E-mini S P 500 and Russell 2000 futures as fund managers navigate window dressing under a new SEC monthly reporting mandate. This regulatory shift could alter typical flow dynamics, amplified by a July 4 holiday-shortened calendar. Beyond structural flows, a massive slate of global employment data takes center stage. The U.S. labor market comes into sharp focus with the ADP monthly employment change and the critical June Nonfarm Payrolls report, which arrives a day early on Thursday. Following a double-digit drop in Treasury yields last week, these key jobs reports will either solidify a new macro…
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Commodities weekly: June reset as peace hopes, hawkish Fed and fund liquidation hit crowded longs
items.excerpt: Key points: The Bloomberg Commodity Total Return Index is heading for a sixth consecutive weekly decline, down around 12% from last month's record high, with weakness broad-based across the sector. The US-Iran peace deal triggered a major repricing across energy and Gulf-dependent commodities, shifting attention from supply disruption to the release of stranded barrels and improved logistics through the Strait of Hormuz. A hawkish Federal Reserve, a resurgent US dollar and renewed concerns about inflation have weighed heavily on precious and industrial metals, while broad hedge fund liquidation has amplified price declines beyond what current fundamentals alone would justify. While the near-term outlook remains challenged by macro headwinds and elevated volatility, the long-term…
items.author: Ole Hansen

items.title: Euro futures climb after testing one-year lows
items.excerpt: Euro futures finished higher on the session, closing at 113.80 after repeatedly testing and bouncing off a one-year low of 113.25. The rebound coincided with a softer U.S. Dollar as morning economic data tempered market expectations for upcoming interest rate hikes. As a result of the recovery, the CVOL index indicated a decline in currency volatility. Looking ahead, the market anticipates inflation sentiment numbers on Friday, followed by a highly anticipated schedule next week featuring the ECB Forum at Sintra with comments from global central bank heads and Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, alongside key U.S. labor data.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Markets digest persistent inflation and upcoming Fed speeches.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino covers the latest market moves as Nasdaq futures hover near the flatline despite upbeat AI guidance. He breaks down the May PCE data, which points to persistent inflation and re-accelerating annual rates, keeping the market focused on the Federal Reserve's 2% target. With upcoming speeches from Neel Kashkari and Christopher Waller, Iaccino discusses how shifting rate expectations might impact the higher for longer narrative. He also previews upcoming earnings from FedEx and Wise Group, alongside key U.S. and international economic data releases.
items.author: CME Group
